majorEmployers.name: Seiling Public Schools
majorEmployers.notes: PreK-12 school district serving northern Dewey County with approximately 429 students

majorEmployers.name: Seiling Municipal Hospital
majorEmployers.notes: 18-bed Critical Access Hospital providing emergency, acute care, swing bed, lab, and radiology services

majorEmployers.name: Great Plains National Bank
majorEmployers.notes: Full-service bank branch serving Dewey County since 1929; part of 26-branch regional banking network

majorEmployers.name: Taloga Wind LLC
majorEmployers.notes: 130-megawatt wind farm with 54 turbines operational since 2011, generating electricity for approximately 35,000 homes

uniqueFacts: Dewey County was originally designated as County D when created in 1891, and was renamed in 1898 to honor Admiral George Dewey after his victory at Manila Bay during the Spanish-American War
The county was part of the Cheyenne-Arapaho Reservation before being opened to non-Indian settlement on April 19, 1892
The 1925 Dewey County Courthouse was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1985; a modern replacement was built in 2009-2010 and the historic building was demolished in 2010
Seiling was once known as the 'Little Louisville of Oklahoma' racing due to its popular horse racing track that operated from the early 1900s through the 1950s
The Taloga Wind Farm generates 130 megawatts of electricity from 54 turbines, making renewable energy a growing contributor to the local economy
processServingNotes: The Dewey County Courthouse is located at 216 Broadway in Taloga. Process servers should check in at the court clerk's office on the north side of the entrance lobby. The courthouse is a single-story modern building constructed in 2009-2010.

Dewey County is part of the 4th Judicial District of Oklahoma, which also includes Alfalfa, Blaine, Garfield, Grant, Kingfisher, Major, Woods, and Woodward counties. Cases filed in Dewey County District Court will have case numbers prefixed with the county designation.

Because Dewey County lies within the Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribal jurisdictional area, process servers may encounter tribal trust land or tribal members. Service of process involving tribal entities or members on trust land may require coordination with tribal courts.
heroLead: Dewey County spans 1,008 square miles of rolling prairie in west-central Oklahoma, where agriculture, oil and gas production, and wind energy drive the local economy. Named for Admiral George Dewey after his 1898 victory at Manila Bay, the county was once part of the Cheyenne-Arapaho Reservation before opening to settlement in 1892. Whether you need process served at the Taloga courthouse, an oilfield office near Seiling, or a ranch along the Canadian River, Just Legal Solutions covers all of Dewey County—call (539) 367-6832 or visit our website to /contact.

faqs.question: Where is the Dewey County Courthouse located and what are the clerk's office hours?
faqs.answer: The Dewey County Courthouse is located at 216 Broadway in Taloga, Oklahoma 73667. The court clerk's office is open Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM and can be reached at 580-328-5521. The courthouse houses the District Court for the 4th Judicial District.

faqs.question: What towns and communities are in Dewey County where legal papers may need to be served?
faqs.answer: Dewey County has seven incorporated towns: Seiling (the largest with about 860 residents), Vici, Leedey, Taloga (the county seat), Camargo, Oakwood, and Putnam. There are also smaller unincorporated communities including Fay and Lenora. Each town has its own municipal court for local ordinance violations.

faqs.question: Does tribal jurisdiction affect process serving in Dewey County?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dewey County falls within the jurisdictional area of the Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribes. Service of process involving tribal members or tribal trust land may require coordination with the Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribal Court in Concho. If you need assistance navigating tribal jurisdictional issues in Dewey County, call Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.

faqs.question: How do I look up case information for Dewey County District Court?
faqs.answer: Dewey County District Court case information is available through the Oklahoma State Courts Network (OSCN) at oscn.net. You can search by case number, party name, or attorney. The OSCN database key for Dewey County is 'dewey'. Cases are filed in the 4th Judicial District.

faqs.question: What are the main industries and employers in Dewey County?
faqs.answer: Dewey County's economy is driven by agriculture (wheat, cattle), oil and gas production, wind energy, and government services. Major employers include Seiling Public Schools, Seiling Municipal Hospital, Great Plains National Bank, and various oilfield service companies. The Taloga Wind Farm also provides local employment.

faqs.question: When was Dewey County founded and how did it get its name?
faqs.answer: Dewey County was created in 1892 and opened to settlement on April 19, 1892. It was originally designated as County D by Congress and renamed Dewey County in November 1898 to honor Admiral George Dewey, following his naval victory at Manila Bay during the Spanish-American War. The county was formerly part of the Cheyenne-Arapaho Reservation. For help with process serving in Dewey County, contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.
